LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT JENTA SHEN MD'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO FEBRUARY 7, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE PLAINTIFF THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. PLAINTIFF MUST IMMEDIATELY DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF ITS OPPOSITION TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOTION AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
MEMO IN OPPOS TO MOTON TO CONTINUE TRIAL FILED BY PLAINTIFF LEWIS, FLORENCE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JENTA SHEN MD'SOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM AGAINST DR. SHEN WAS NOT LIMITED TO THE SURGERY. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANTS NEGLIGENTLY PERFORMED THE SURGERY AND THE TREATMENT. "SURGERY" AND "TREATMENT" ARE EACH DISTINCTLY PLED. SEE, E.G., PARA. 21. ("[T]HAT THE TREATMENT AND SURGERY PERFORMED BY DEFENDANTS, AND EACH OF THEM. . . NEGLIGENTLY FAILED TO CONFORM TO THE STANDARD OF CARE BOTH WITH RESPECT TO THE CARE AND TREATMENT RENDERED TO PLAINTIFF, AND WITH RESPECT TO PROVIDING TO PLAINTIFF INFORMATION ABOUT THE RISKS AND HAZARDS, OR OTHER HARMFUL CONSEQUENCES, THAT MIGHT FOLLOW FROM THE TREATMENT, DIAGNOSIS OR SURGERY THE DEFENDANTS, AND EACH OF THEM, PLANNED AND PERFORMED FOR PLAINTIFF.") FURTHER, THE COMPLAINT STATES THAT "SUCH FAILURE TO REMOVE ALL OR PART OF THE CERVIX, AND OTHER ACTS AND OMISSIONS BY THE DEFENDANTS, CONSTITUTE A BREACH OF THE APPLICABLE STANDARD OF CARE." PURSUANT TO CCP 452, THE COMPLAINT PLEADS A THEORY OF LIABILITY BASED UPON THE RELATED TREATMENT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.
DECLARATION OF FRANCES BURNS, ESQ. FILED BY DEFENDANT SHEN MD, JENTA

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-07-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DAVID KLECZEK, ESQ., 510.663.7100, FOR PLAINTIFF FLORENCE LEWIS.
FRANCES BURNS, ESQ., 415.397.2800, FOR DEFENDANT JENTA SHEN M.D.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT JENTA SHEN MD'SOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied because defendant fails to shift the burden. Plaintiff's claim against Dr. Shen was not limited to the surgery. The Complaint alleges that defendants negligently performed the surgery and the treatment. "Surgery" and "treatment" are each distinctly pled. See, e.g., Para. 21. ("[t]hat the treatment and surgery performed by Defendants, and each of them. . . negligently failed to conform to the standard of care both with respect to the care and treatment rendered to plaintiff, and with respect to providing to Plaintiff information about the risks and hazards, or other harmful consequences, that might follow from the treatment, diagnosis or surgery the Defendants, and each of them, planned and performed for Plaintiff.") Further, the Complaint states that "such failure to remove all or part of the cervix, and other acts and omissions by the Defendants, constitute a breach of the applicable standard of care." Pursuant to CCP 452, the complaint pleads a theory of liability based upon the related treatment. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
